forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KW26BJ1GQ9MJ8YAX83J5548P
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c180
+project: expect
+title: 'gui surface adapter (OS a11y: AX/UIA/AT-SPI) incl. Tauri'
+---
+## What
+Add the `gui` surface: drive and observe native desktop apps through the OS accessibility API — including Tauri/Electron via the bridged webview a11y tree. Per `ideas/expect.md` §"Surface adapters" (gui row) and §"Drilling into a Tauri / Electron app".
+
+- New `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surface/gui.rs` implementing `SurfaceAdapter`, per-OS in-process mechanism:
+  - **macOS**: AX (`AXUIElement`), drive via `AXUIElementPerformAction`/`AXPress`, observe the AX subtree (Tauri WKWebView appears under an `AXWebArea`).
+  - **Windows**: UIA (`IUIAutomation`), `InvokePattern` (WebView2 bridged; bonus CDP escape hatch noted, not required).
+  - **Linux**: AT-SPI (`atspi` + `zbus`), bridged for WebKitGTK.
+  - Drive/observe by `role[name=…]` through the same API; locator dialect identical to browser (a11y-stable, not pixels). Reuse the a11y locator resolution from the browser task.
+  - Provision launches the app; teardown closes it.
+- Validate against the in-repo `kanban-app` (Tauri): `surface: gui`, native a11y, NO CDP/Node — macOS AX reads the bridged React tree and `AXPress` actuates it. Quality caveat: the bridged tree is only as good as the web app's semantics.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] On the host OS, the adapter launches a native app, presses a control by `role[name=…]`, and snapshots the resulting a11y tree.
+- [ ] A Tauri app's bridged web content appears as real a11y nodes (e.g. a `<button aria-label>` shows as a named `button`); the adapter drives it with no CDP/Node.
+- [ ] Locator binding/drift behaves like the browser surface (rename ⇒ structural drift).
+- [ ] Teardown closes the app.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Integration test on the CI host OS (macOS runner) driving a minimal native/Tauri fixture (or `kanban-app`) by role+name and asserting an observed node. Gate per-OS; document skips on unsupported OS.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect gui` passes (or skips cleanly off-platform).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`. macOS AX first (the CI runner is a Mac); Windows/Linux behind cfg.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,28 +6,27 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: c180
 project: expect
-title: 'gui surface adapter (OS a11y: AX/UIA/AT-SPI) incl. Tauri'
+title: 'gui surface adapter: macOS AX + Tauri bridge (testable core)'
 ---
 ## What
-Add the `gui` surface: drive and observe native desktop apps through the OS accessibility API — including Tauri/Electron via the bridged webview a11y tree. Per `ideas/expect.md` §"Surface adapters" (gui row) and §"Drilling into a Tauri / Electron app".
+Add the `gui` surface for macOS — drive and observe native desktop apps through the OS accessibility API (AX), including Tauri webview content via the bridged a11y tree. This is the testable core (the CI runner is a Mac). Windows/Linux are a separate follow-on task. Per `ideas/expect.md` §"Surface adapters" (gui row) and §"Drilling into a Tauri / Electron app".

-- New `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surface/gui.rs` implementing `SurfaceAdapter`, per-OS in-process mechanism:
-  - **macOS**: AX (`AXUIElement`), drive via `AXUIElementPerformAction`/`AXPress`, observe the AX subtree (Tauri WKWebView appears under an `AXWebArea`).
-  - **Windows**: UIA (`IUIAutomation`), `InvokePattern` (WebView2 bridged; bonus CDP escape hatch noted, not required).
-  - **Linux**: AT-SPI (`atspi` + `zbus`), bridged for WebKitGTK.
-  - Drive/observe by `role[name=…]` through the same API; locator dialect identical to browser (a11y-stable, not pixels). Reuse the a11y locator resolution from the browser task.
+- New `crates/swissarmyhammer-expect/src/surface/gui.rs` implementing `SurfaceAdapter` for macOS:
+  - macOS **AX** (`AXUIElement`): drive via `AXUIElementPerformAction`/`AXPress`, observe the AX subtree. A Tauri WKWebView's web content appears under an `AXWebArea` (e.g. a `<button aria-label>` shows as a named `button`) — no CDP/Node needed.
+  - Drive/observe by `role[name=…]`; reuse the a11y locator dialect + resolution from the browser task (identical, a11y-stable, not pixels). A control rename ⇒ structural drift.
   - Provision launches the app; teardown closes it.
-- Validate against the in-repo `kanban-app` (Tauri): `surface: gui`, native a11y, NO CDP/Node — macOS AX reads the bridged React tree and `AXPress` actuates it. Quality caveat: the bridged tree is only as good as the web app's semantics.
+  - Structure the OS backend behind a small per-OS trait/seam so the Windows/Linux follow-on slots in cleanly behind `cfg`.
+- Validate against the in-repo `kanban-app` (Tauri): `surface: gui`, native AX, NO CDP/Node. Quality caveat: the bridged tree is only as good as the web app's semantics.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] On the host OS, the adapter launches a native app, presses a control by `role[name=…]`, and snapshots the resulting a11y tree.
-- [ ] A Tauri app's bridged web content appears as real a11y nodes (e.g. a `<button aria-label>` shows as a named `button`); the adapter drives it with no CDP/Node.
+- [ ] On macOS, the adapter launches a native/Tauri app, presses a control by `role[name=…]`, and snapshots the resulting AX tree.
+- [ ] A Tauri app's bridged web content appears as real AX nodes; the adapter drives it with no CDP/Node.
 - [ ] Locator binding/drift behaves like the browser surface (rename ⇒ structural drift).
-- [ ] Teardown closes the app.
+- [ ] Teardown closes the app; the per-OS seam is in place (Windows/Linux are `unimplemented`/cfg-gated stubs, not built here).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Integration test on the CI host OS (macOS runner) driving a minimal native/Tauri fixture (or `kanban-app`) by role+name and asserting an observed node. Gate per-OS; document skips on unsupported OS.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect gui` passes (or skips cleanly off-platform).
+- [ ] Integration test on the macOS CI runner driving a minimal Tauri fixture (or `kanban-app`) by role+name and asserting an observed AX node. Gate to macOS; skip cleanly off-platform.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-expect gui` passes on macOS (or skips cleanly off-platform).

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd`. macOS AX first (the CI runner is a Mac); Windows/Linux behind cfg.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd`.
\ No newline at end of file

+  - **macOS**: AX (`AXUIElement`), drive via `AXUIElementPerformAction`/`AXPress`, observe the AX subtree (Tauri WKWebView appears under an `AXWebArea`).
